ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re Contempt
MINI MINUTES:
On June 3, 2024, this court issued, after noticed ex parte hearing, Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee ("Order"). Paragraph Four of the order states: "Respondent Mark Borsuk is ordered to immediately deliver to professional private fiduciary David A. Bradlow all assets and records of the Trust Created Under the Last Will and Testament of Etta Bacharach."
On March 20, 2026, following hearing on Temporary Trustee David A. Bradlow's Motion to Compel Compliance and for Order to Show Cause Why Respondent Mark Borsuk Should Not Be Held in Contempt, the court concurrently issued two orders: Order After Hearing On Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Order To Show Cause Re Contempt To Issue; and Order To Show Cause Re Contempt.
In the Order to Show Cause, Mark Borsuk was ordered to show cause why he should not be held in contempt for failing to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee, and fined $1,000 and ordered to pay the reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred by temporary trustee David A. Bradlow in connection with the contempt proceeding, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sec. 1218(a).
Mr. Borsuk timely filed a response to the Order to Show Cause. Temporary Trustee David A. Bradlow filed a reply to the response, with supporting declaration of counsel.
Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1209(a)(5), disobedience of any lawful order of the court is a contempt of the authority of the court.
The court finds as follows: (1) the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee was a valid order; (2) Mark Borsuk had actual knowledge of the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee; (3) Mark Borsuk was able to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee; (4) Mark Borsuk willfully failed to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee.
The court finds Mark Borsuk to be GUILTY OF CONTEMPT, for willfully and knowingly failing to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee.
Pursuant to the court's powers and discretion under Code of Civil Procedure section 1218, the court sanctions Mark Borsuk in the amount of $250, payable to the Clerk of the Court within ten days of entry of this order. Further, pursuant to its discretion under Code of Civil Procedure section 1218(a), the court awards $4,000 in reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in connection with the contempt proceeding to Temporary Trustee David A. Bradlow. Mark Borsuk shall pay to Temporary Trustee David A. Bradlow the amount of $4,000 within ten days of entry of this order.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John C. Clark for Sheila Siegel and Suzanne Borzuk (via Zoom).
Jeremiah Harvey for Mark Borzuk (via Zoom).
Fredrick Crombie for trustee David Bradlow (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on petitions, accountings, and trial setting.

The Court inquired regarding the status of the storage unit and turnover of records. Mr. Crombie reported that the key had been delivered, access obtained, and that he and the trustee were working on a plan to review and handle the records.

The Court then addressed the status of the underlying matters and trial readiness. Mr. Harvey reported that additional depositions remained and that he and Mr. Clark had been engaged in discussions that may narrow the issues. Mr. Harvey and Mr. Clark requested that a trial date be set later in 2026. Mr. Crombie deferred to other counsel.

The Court inquired regarding compliance with the June 3, 2024 order requiring turnover of all trust assets and records. Petition For Order 1)Removing Trustee & Surcharge 2)Compelling Trustee To Account 3)Reducing Trustee'S Use Of Trust Funds For Litigation And 5)Awarding Damages And Attorneys' Fee And Costs For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. PES-66-178094, Estate of Etta Bacharach.

Appearances via Zoom: James Cummins, Esq. and Chelsea Suttman, Esq. for Sheila Siegel and Suzanne Borsuk (Petitioners). Tad Devlin, Esq. and Emmalee McCarthy, Esq. for Mark Borsuk (Respondent/Trustee).

Mr. Cummins stated that the petition was filed in the estate case no. PES-06-178094 and also filed in the trust case no. PTR-22-305483. The Court ordered the same petition in case no. PES-06-178094 off calendar.

Mr. Cummins argued in support of suspending the trustee stating that their request for information dating back to March is still not satisfied and that there is a lack of information to the beneficiaries. Mr. Cummins stated that the trustee should be suspended, the he should be ordered to account in 60 days, that the matter should be set for trial, and that there should be a removal, a surcharge, and a fiduciary appointed by the Court. Mr. Devlin stated that they are just joining the case, that the trustee is being fully transparent and actively handling affairs, and that there has been no use of estate funds. Mr. Devlin also stated that there are active discussions about mediation but this is not as simple as a division of property, that they are in the process of preparing written responses, that they are responding to counsel's prior request for information, that there is no co-mingling, and that the removal is not needed. The Court inquired about a distribution in kind/distribution of shares. Mr. Devlin stated that it could be discussed in mediation. Mr. Cummins stated that the accounting should be code compliant, that it is not based on the date of death, that the trust holds nine income generating properties, that they want date-of-death appraisals, and that they are necessary for the estate tax returns. The Court inquired about what's required under the code. Brief discussions are held. Mr. Cummins stated that they need to see the transactions in the other accounts, that there is an issue as to the Chestnut property, and that the trustee is using his real estate license for self-interest. The Court stated that there are no issues of fact at this point, that everyone should take a fresh approach to this, that this is a new opportunity, and that it wants counsel to meet and confer on ADR prior to the next calling of the matter.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court ordered the trustee to provide an account for the two-year period of: 2/7/19 through 2/6/21. The accounting is to be code compliant. The Court denied Mr. Cummins request for the trustee to provide an accounting for his full term. The Court noted that it appreciates what has been added here today and ordered counsel to meet and confer on ADR/how to proceed on the pending petition.

The Petition for Order 1) Removing Trustee & Surcharge 2) Compelling Trustee to Account 3) Reducing Trustee's Use of Trust Funds for Litigation and 5) Awarding Damages and Attorney's Fee and Costs for Financial Elder Abuse is continued to March 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Status on the filing of the accounting is also set for March 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.
